message:
perf(btree): widen CachedCellSlots inline capacity and inline cursor stack (bd-9e3xf.2)

Follow-up to the bd-9e3xf cell-slot cache-miss audit, which classified
cache misses into COLD / INVALIDATED / SLOT buckets and showed
MISS_SLOT at 71 % of all misses — 54,739 / 76,865 across the workload
suite. Nearly all MISS_SLOT observations come from index-btree binary
search on a just-invalidated leaf: a search on an ~N-cell leaf adds
log2(N)+1 fresh entries as each probe misses.

With the previous inline capacity of 8 slots, any leaf wider than
~256 cells spilled to the heap on the 9th probe, paying a malloc
plus copy per search — directly on the hot index-seek path.

Changes:

- CachedCellSlots: widen SmallVec inline capacity 8 → 16 in the type
  alias. Sixteen slots cover leaves up to ~65 k cells inline (each
  added slot doubles coverage via log2). Memory cost is bounded at
  +8 slots × 56 bytes ≈ 450 bytes per entry, capped at
  CELL_SLOT_CACHE_ENTRIES = 64 entries per cursor — so worst case
  is ~28 KB extra per cursor, paid only by cursors that actually
  populated all 64 entries.

- BtCursor::stack: migrate from Vec<StackEntry> to a SmallVec-backed
  CursorStack with CURSOR_STACK_INLINE_DEPTH = 4. Most cursor
  lifetimes stay within root / interior / leaf depth, and the prepared
  direct-insert append lane can finish without ever pushing a stack
  entry. A 4-slot inline buffer keeps shallow descents heap-free while
  deeper btrees still spill gracefully.

No observable-behavior or serialization changes; these are pure
inline-capacity and stack-allocation tuning levers backed by the
bd-9e3xf audit.
